title: Thriprayar Ramaswamy Temple
tagline: Thriprayar Ramaswamy Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Thriprayar Shree Ramaswami Temple is Hindu temple situated in Triprayar in Thrissur district of Kerala state in India.
overview.description: Thriprayar Shree Ramaswami Temple is Hindu temple situated in Triprayar in Thrissur district of Kerala state in India. The deity is Rama, the seventh incarnation of Vishnu, with four arms bearing a conch, a discus, a bow, and a garland. The temple is situated on the bank of 'Thirupurayar' which means The Sacred River in front of Temple. Thirupurayar pronounced as 'Triprayar' during the course of time (decades Or centuries) This canal later developed as Inland Water Route in end of 18th Century.Canoli Canal, which connects Kozhikode and Kodungallur.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Triprayar
topPlaces.description: Thriprayar is a town in Thrissur District of Kerala, India. It is famous for the Thriprayar Temple.

topPlaces.name: Chemmappilly
topPlaces.description: Chemmappilly is a place in Thrissur District, Kerala, 23 km from Thrissur. It is in the jurisdiction of Anthikad Police station and the Grama Panchayath is Thanniyam.

topPlaces.name: Sreeraman Chira
topPlaces.description: Sreeraman Chira is a fresh water lake which contains 900 hectares of paddy fields situated in Chemmappilly near Triprayar of Thrissur district of Kerala state in India. The lake is linked to Ramayana fame, where the Chira kettal ceremony reminiscent of the Sethubandhanam where Hanuman and his army built Rama Setu to reach Sri Lanka and rescue Sita from the Rakshasa King, Ravana.

topPlaces.name: Sethubandhanam at Sreeraman Chira Chemmappilly
topPlaces.description: Sethubandhanam at Sreeraman Chira or Chira kettal is a Hindu ceremony reminiscent of the Sethubandhanam where Hanuman and his army built Rama Setu to reach Sri Lanka and rescue Sita from the Rakshasa King, Ravana. It is annually conducted at Sreeraman Chira, a 900 hectares of paddy field situated in Chemmappilly near Triprayar in the Thrissur district of Kerala state in India.

topPlaces.name: Peringottukara
topPlaces.description: Peringottukara is a village in the western coastal side of India, located on the western side of Thrissur district, Kerala. The village is much famous for Vishnumaya Temples.

topPlaces.name: Vadakkummuri
topPlaces.description: Vadakkummuri is a village in Thrissur district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Aneswaram Siva Temple
topPlaces.description: Aneswaram Siva temple is at Chemmappilly, the western area of Thrissur District. Its main Deity is Shiva.

topPlaces.name: Thanniyam
topPlaces.description: Thanniyam is a village in Thrissur district in the state of Kerala, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Thriprayar Ramaswamy Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Thriprayar Ramaswamy Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Thriprayar Ramaswamy Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (43 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (53 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Thriprayar Ramaswamy Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Thriprayar Ramaswamy Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Thriprayar Ramaswamy Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Thriprayar Ramaswamy Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Thriprayar Ramaswamy Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Triprayar, Chemmappilly, Sreeraman Chira, Sethubandhanam at Sreeraman Chira Chemmappilly, Peringottukara.
